spots.name: Konark Sun Temple
spots.description: The Konark Sun Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is renowned for its magnificent chariot-shaped architecture dedicated to the Sun God. The temple made by Langula Narasimha Dev, named as 'Black Pagoda'.

spots.name: Jagannath Temple
spots.description: One of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites, famous for Lord Jagannath and the annual Rath Yatra festival. The presence of lord Jagannath makes Odisha graceful, so the land is called as 'Shree Kshetra.'

spots.name: Nandankanan Zoo. Park
spots.description: India's famous zoological park known for its white tigers, lion safari and wildlife conservation programs. Home to the world's first captive-bred White Tiger and spread across a scenic forest and lake ecosystem, offering a unique wildlife experience in Odisha.

spots.name: Chilika Lake
spots.description: Asia's largest brackish water lagoon famous for migratory birds, dolphins and scenic beauty. Also home to the revered Kalijai Temple, a sacred island shrine dedicated to Goddess Kalijai, attracting thousands of devotees and visitors throughout the year.

spots.name: Daringbadi
spots.description: Known as the 'Kashmir of Odisha', Daringbadi is famous for pine forests, waterfalls and cool climate. Nestled in the Eastern Ghats, it is one of the few places in Odisha that occasionally experiences frost during winter, making it a popular hill station and nature getaway.

spots.name: Lingaraj Temple
spots.description: A masterpiece of Kalinga architecture dedicated to Lord Shiva and one of Odisha's most sacred temples. The Lingaraj Temple was built in the 11th century by King Jajati Keshari of the Somavamsi dynasty which is is renowned for it's towering spire & intricate stone carvings.

spots.name: Similipal National Park
spots.description: A UNESCO Biosphere Reserve famous for dense forests, waterfalls, tigers & elephants. Spread across the Eastern Ghats, Similipal is one of India's largest protected forest ecosystems, renowned for its rich biodiversity, scenic landscapes, and indigenous tribal heritage.

spots.name: Deomali Hills
spots.description: The highest mountain peak in Odisha offering breathtaking sunrise views, trekking and adventure opportunities. Nestled in the Eastern Ghats of Koraput district, Deomali is surrounded by lush green valleys, rolling meadows, and mist-covered landscapes, making it a paradise for nature lovers and photographers.

spots.name: Gopalpur Beach
spots.description: A serene beach destination known for golden sands, sea breeze and spectacular sunsets. Once a bustling colonial-era port town, Gopalpur Beach offers a perfect blend of natural beauty, peaceful ambiance, and water-based activities, making it one of Odisha's most popular coastal getaways.

spots.name: Ratnagiri Buddhist Complex
spots.description: An important Buddhist archaeological site featuring ancient monasteries, stupas and stone sculptures. Part of Odisha's renowned Diamond Triangle of Buddhist heritage, Ratnagiri flourished as a major center of Mahayana and Vajrayana Buddhism between the 5th and 13th centuries.

spots.name: Tara Tarini Temple
spots.description: One of the oldest Shakti Peethas of India, Which is perched atop Kumari Hills overlooking the Rushikulya River. Dedicated to the twin goddesses Tara & Tarini, the temple is a revered pilgrimage destination known for it's spiritual significance, scenic surroundings & grand Chaitra Yatra festival.

spots.name: Barabati Fort
spots.description: A historic fort built by the Eastern Ganga dynasty, Barabati Fort stands as a symbol of Odisha's medieval military architecture. Located in Cuttack, the fort is famous for its impressive nine-storeyed gateway ruins, moat system, and rich historical legacy dating back to the 14th century.

spots.name: Biraja Temple
spots.description: Dedicated to Goddess Biraja, this ancient temple is one of Odisha's most sacred pilgrimage sites and an important Shakti Peetha. Located in Jajpur, the temple is revered as the abode of Goddess Biraja, attracting thousands of devotees and preserving centuries of spiritual and cultural heritage.

spots.name: Chandipur Beach
spots.description: The beach is renowned for its rare receding shoreline (Vanishing Sea Phenomena), where the sea retreats up to 5 kilometers during low tide, and for hosting the Missile Launch Centre at the Integrated Test Range, a key hub for India's missile development and testing programs.

spots.name: Wonderla Amusement Park
spots.description: A modern amusement and water park offering thrilling rides, family attractions and exciting entertainment experiences. One of Odisha's newest leisure destinations, Wonderla features world-class rides, water slides, and interactive attractions, making it a popular getaway for families and adventure seekers alike.

spots.name: Dhauli Shanti Stupa
spots.description: Built on the historic Kalinga War site, Dhauli Shanti Stupa symbolizes peace and marks Emperor Ashoka's transformation towards Buddhism. The Dhauli Shanti Stupa, also known as the Peace Pagoda, was constructed by the Japan Buddha Sangha and the Kalinga Nippon Buddha Sangha in 1972.

spots.name: Tarini Temple
spots.description: One of Odisha's most revered Shakti shrines, Maa Tarini Temple attracts thousands of devotees throughout the year. Situated atop a small hill in Ghatagaon, the temple is especially famous for devotees offering coconuts tied with red cloth as a symbol of their wishes.

spots.name: Hirakud Dam
spots.description: Built across the Mahanadi River, Hirakud Dam is one of the world's longest earthen dams. It offers breathtaking reservoir views, scenic gardens, watch towers and serves as a major source of irrigation and hydroelectric power.

spots.name: Sanaghagara Waterfall
spots.description: A picturesque waterfall surrounded by lush forests, Sanaghagara is one of Keonjhar's most popular picnic destinations. The cascading water and serene environment make it ideal for nature lovers.

spots.name: Gundichaghagi Waterfall
spots.description: Nestled amidst dense forests near Ghatagaon, Gundichaghagi Waterfall is known for its crystal-clear waters, tranquil surroundings and scenic trekking trails.

spots.name: Byas Sarovar
spots.description: A sacred water tank believed to be associated with Sage Ved Vyasa, Byas Sarovar is an important pilgrimage site and a prominent landmark in the historic town of Jajpur.

spots.name: Khandagiri Caves
spots.description: These ancient rock-cut caves were carved during the reign of Emperor Kharavela and once served as residences for Jain monks. They are among Odisha's most significant archaeological monuments.

spots.name: Udayagiri Caves
spots.description: Located opposite Khandagiri, the Udayagiri Caves feature beautifully carved chambers, inscriptions and sculptures dating back to the 2nd century BCE, reflecting Odisha's rich Jain heritage.

spots.name: Jirang Buddhist Monastery
spots.description: Also known as Padmasambhava Mahavihara, it is the largest Buddhist monastery in Eastern India. The monastery showcases magnificent Tibetan architecture amidst peaceful surroundings.

spots.name: Taptapani
spots.description: A popular hill destination famous for its natural hot sulphur springs, lush forests and scenic beauty. The therapeutic spring water attracts visitors throughout the year.

spots.name: Harishankar Temple
spots.description: A sacred shrine nestled in the Gandhamardan Hills, Harishankar Temple is dedicated to both Lord Vishnu and Lord Shiva and is renowned for its beautiful waterfall and trekking trails.

spots.name: Kapilas Temple
spots.description: Dedicated to Lord Chandrasekhar (Shiva), Kapilas Temple sits atop the scenic Kapilas Hills at an elevation of about 2,239 feet. Devotees climb its 1,352 steps while enjoying breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding forests.

spots.name: Duduma Waterfall
spots.description: One of India's tallest waterfalls, Duduma plunges about 157 meters from the Machkund River amidst lush Eastern Ghats. It is also home to the historic Machkund Hydroelectric Project.

spots.name: Manikeswari Temple
spots.description: Dedicated to Goddess Manikeswari, the presiding deity of the former Kalahandi kingdom, this ancient temple is famous for its grand Chhatar Yatra festival celebrated every Dussehra.

spots.name: Khirachora Gopinath Temple
spots.description: A revered Vaishnavite temple dedicated to Lord Krishna, famous for the legend of 'Kshira Chora Gopinath' and its sacred sweet offering, Amruta Keli.

spots.name: Emami Jagannath Temple
spots.description: A magnificent modern temple inspired by the architecture of the Jagannath Temple in Puri. It has become a prominent spiritual landmark and tourist attraction in Bhubaneswar.

spots.name: Tikarpada
spots.description: Situated within Satkosia Tiger Reserve, Tikarpada is renowned for its spectacular Mahanadi Gorge, crocodile conservation centre, boating and riverside eco-tourism experiences.

spots.name: Gahirmatha Marine Sanctuary
spots.description: The world's largest nesting ground for Olive Ridley sea turtles, Gahirmatha Marine Sanctuary is one of India's most significant marine conservation areas.

spots.name: Debrigarh Wildlife Sanctuary
spots.description: Overlooking the Hirakud Reservoir, Debrigarh Wildlife Sanctuary is home to leopards, elephants, bison, deer and numerous bird species, making it one of Odisha's premier eco-tourism destinations.

spots.name: Aradi Akhandalamani Temple
spots.description: Dedicated to Lord Akhandalamani (Shiva), this sacred riverside temple on the banks of the Baitarani River is one of the most revered pilgrimage sites in northern Odisha.

spots.name: Mangalajodi
spots.description: Located on the northern edge of Chilika Lake, Mangalajodi is internationally renowned for community-led bird conservation and offers unforgettable boat safaris to observe thousands of migratory birds.

spots.name: Dhabaleswar Temple
spots.description: Dedicated to Lord Shiva, Dhabaleswar Temple is situated on a picturesque island in the Mahanadi River and is famous for its iconic suspension bridge and the grand Bada Osha festival.

spots.name: Mahendragiri
spots.description: One of Odisha's highest mountain peaks, Mahendragiri is renowned for trekking, camping, spectacular sunrise views and its mythological association with Lord Parashurama and the Pandavas.

spots.name: Jhankad Sarala Temple
spots.description: Dedicated to Goddess Sarala, the temple is an important Shakti shrine and is closely associated with Adikabi Sarala Das, who is believed to have received divine blessings here.

spots.name: Bhitarkanika National Park
spots.description: India's second-largest mangrove ecosystem, Bhitarkanika National Park is famous for its saltwater crocodiles, rich biodiversity, mangrove forests and scenic boat cruises.

spots.name: Chitrakonda
spots.description: Often called the 'Kashmir of Odisha', Chitrakonda is known for its breathtaking reservoir, picturesque boat rides, lush hills and tranquil natural surroundings.

spots.name: Khiching
spots.description: An ancient temple town renowned for the Kichakeswari Temple and exquisite black chlorite stone sculptures that represent Odisha's rich architectural heritage.

spots.name: Kotsamalai (Patali Jagannath Pitha)
spots.description: A sacred shrine where the idols of Lord Jagannath are believed to have been secretly worshipped during invasions, making it one of Odisha's most historically significant Jagannath pilgrimage sites.

spots.name: Fategarh Jagannath Temple
spots.description: Nestled amidst the scenic Fategarh Ghati, this beautiful Jagannath Temple is renowned for its peaceful surroundings, lush greenery and panoramic hill views.

spots.name: Maa Samaleswari Temple
spots.description: Dedicated to Goddess Samaleswari, the presiding deity of Western Odisha, this centuries-old temple is the spiritual and cultural heart of the Sambalpur region.

spots.name: Khandadhar Waterfall
spots.description: Odisha's highest waterfall, cascading from a height of about 244 meters, Khandadhar is surrounded by dense forests and offers spectacular trekking and nature photography opportunities.

spots.name: Malayagiri Hill
spots.description: The highest mountain peak of Angul district, Malayagiri Hill is a paradise for trekkers, offering dense forests, scenic trails, rich biodiversity and panoramic views of the Eastern Ghats.

spots.name: Sabara Srikhetra
spots.description: A magnificent Jagannath Temple inspired by the Puri shrine, Sabara Srikhetra celebrates the tribal connection of Lord Jagannath and serves as a major spiritual and cultural landmark of southern Odisha.

spots.name: Wonderland Water Park
spots.description: One of Odisha's popular family entertainment destinations, Wonderland Water Park features thrilling water slides, wave pools, rain dance attractions and fun-filled recreational activities for visitors of all ages.

spots.name: Satkosia Forest Reserve
spots.description: A spectacular tiger reserve and wildlife sanctuary spread across the Mahanadi Gorge, Satkosia is renowned for its dense forests, crocodile conservation, elephants, leopards, birdlife and unforgettable jungle safaris.
